codeReview.summary: The feature adds reply/thread functionality with broad test coverage, but a dedupe replay edge case breaks causal linkage guarantees by returning impossible reply metadata.

issues.description: `replyMessage` replays any existing row by `dedupe_key` without ensuring it is a compatible reply, then fabricates `in_reply_to` via `existing.in_reply_to ?? parentMessageId`. If the dedupe key was previously used by a root `send`, reply returns that root message ID while reporting it as a reply (including self-referential linkage in practice), violating VAL-THREAD-001 causal linkage correctness. Repro: send with `--dedupe-key dup_shared`, then reply to that message with the same dedupe key; returned JSON shows the original message ID with `dedupe_replay:true` and invalid reply linkage semantics.

The suite section named 'concurrent replies' executes replies sequentially (no parallel/process-level contention), so it does not meaningfully validate concurrency behavior under race conditions. This leaves a coverage gap for the explicit concurrency requirement in VAL-THREAD-003.

summary: Review failed due to a blocking dedupe replay bug in `replyMessage` that can return non-reply records as replies and break causal linkage metadata.
